courses.name: Introduction to Business
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the fundamental principles, functions, and dynamics of business in both national and global contexts. It also seeks to help students understand how businesses operate across key functional areas such as management, marketing, finance, production, and human resources, while emphasizing ethical decision-making, corporate social responsibility, entrepreneurship, sustainability, innovation, and digital transformation.
courses.description: The course provides an overview of business structure, management and organization, human resources, financial management, production, labor-management relations, marketing, accounting, global dimensions of business, and corporate social responsibilities.
learning_outcomes: Explains basic business terms accurately.
Evaluates the effects of national and international business environments on business.
Identifies the main functions of business such as production, marketing, finance, accounting, and human resources.
Applies basic business principles to real-life scenarios.
Discusses ethical business practices and corporate social responsibility concepts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course
Hafta 2: Developing a business mindset
Hafta 3: Economics and businesses
Hafta 4: Global marketplace - 1
Hafta 5: Global marketplace - 2
Hafta 6: Business ethics
Hafta 7: Corporate social responsibility
Hafta 8: Types of business ownership
Hafta 9: Entrepreneurship and small business ownership
Hafta 10: Management and managerial roles
Hafta 11: Management functions and skills
Hafta 12: Group behaviors
Hafta 13: Teamwork
Hafta 14: The future of business
resources: Bovee, C.L. & Thill, J. V. (2024). Business in Action, 10th Global Edition, New Jersey: Prentice Hall.
Forbes, Business Week, Bloomberg, Capital ve Harvard Business Review gibi süreli yayınlar ve dergiler.
Boone, L. E., Kurtz, D. L. (2016). Contemporary Business. USA: Thomson South-Western Corporation.

courses.name: Business Mathematics I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce fundamental mathematical concepts needed to analyze problems in business and economics and to develop quantitative models using these concepts. It also seeks to help students gain mathematical thinking and problem-solving skills that support business decision-making through topics such as functions, limits, continuity, and derivatives.
courses.description: This course covers core calculus topics such as functions and graphs, limits and continuity, and differentiation techniques. It teaches how mathematical tools are used in modeling business and economics problems and in analyzing these models. Students gain the ability to perform applications such as marginal analysis and revenue-cost analysis using the concept of derivatives.
learning_outcomes: Defines basic types of functions and represents them graphically.
Explains the concepts of limits and continuity in the context of business and economics.
Constructs mathematical models for business problems.
Uses differentiation techniques to perform marginal analysis of economic variables.
Interprets mathematical model results and integrates them into business decision-making processes.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions
Hafta 2: Graphs and Transformations
Hafta 3: Linear and Quadratic Functions
Hafta 4: Polynomial and Rational Functions
Hafta 5: Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
Hafta 6: Introduction to Limits
Hafta 7: Infinite Limits and Limits at Infinity
Hafta 8: Continuity
Hafta 9: Derivatives and Basic Differentiation Properties
Hafta 10: Differentials and Marginal Analysis in Business and Economics
Hafta 11: The Constant e, Continuous Compound Interest, Derivatives of Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
Hafta 12: Product and Quotient Rules
Hafta 13: Chain Rule
Hafta 14: Implicit Differentiation

courses.name: ACADEMIC ENGLISH I
courses.purpose: The course aims to develop students' academic English skills, including reading and understanding academic texts, summarizing, quoting, listening to lectures, taking notes, and giving presentations. It also helps students better understand academic texts and use note-taking and presentation skills effectively.
courses.description: This course is designed for first-year students and is the first part of a two-course sequence. It is a process- and skills-based course that aims to help students acquire basic academic English skills. The course develops reading, listening, writing, and note-taking skills through important academic reading passages, and also shows how to focus on preparing academic presentations.
learning_outcomes: Find the main idea in an academic text
Find supporting details in an academic text
Use synonyms to express the main idea or supporting ideas in an academic text
Use synonyms to state the main idea or supporting ideas in an academic text
Identify the purpose and structure of an academic text
Formulate the relationship between sections of a lecture
Use note-taking while listening to lectures to complete missing parts in review and discussion
Know how to organize a presentation
Show the relationship between ideas using logical connectors
Reveal the organizational structure with logical connectors on a specific topic
Take notes from a text and prepare a presentation for class
Follow coherence and cohesion used in academic texts easily
Recognize discourse markers in lectures to create coherence
Understand the main idea and supporting ideas of a lecture
Use different grammatical forms to quote main ideas and key points in a text or recording
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Recognizing the course objectives and classroom rules; Academic Listening (Unit 1): understanding the main idea and introduction in a presentation; identifying word groups; recognizing formulaic expressions needed in presentations; Academic Speaking (Unit 1): preparing for and delivering a presentation; preparing personal information; introducing short presentations; evaluating a presentation

Hafta 2: Academic Speaking: presentation; presenting information about your academic experience and goals; Academic Listening (Unit 2): understanding an introduction to an academic lecture; taking notes on key information; Academic Listening (Unit 3): understanding the main points of a lecture; understanding the organization of a lecture; taking notes on key points; taking detailed notes on examples and explanations; recognizing formulaic expressions

Hafta 3: Academic Listening: giving a presentation; students' presentation recordings; Academic Reading (Unit 4): identifying main ideas in a reading passage; identifying and evaluating supporting evidence in a paragraph; Academic Speaking (Unit 4): taking notes on the main points of a presentation; taking notes on numerical information; presenting numbers; recognizing and using formulaic expressions
Hafta 4: Academic Speaking (Unit 4): giving a short individual presentation on change; peer feedback

Hafta 5: Academic Listening (Unit 5): reflecting on note-taking methods; defending ideas in a discussion; identifying supporting ideas; using past tense to describe research results; Academic Speaking (Unit 5): taking notes on key information; using formal/academic verbs for ideas in a presentation; preparing a presentation to support an idea
Hafta 6: Review for the midterm exam; midterm exam
Hafta 7: Academic Speaking (Unit 6): referring to the main ideas in a passage; using formulaic expressions for reference; reading for a presentation to respond to ideas and suggest ideas

Hafta 8: Academic Reading (Unit 3): identifying the purpose of a passage; understanding main ideas; identifying and using cohesive language; Academic Speaking (Unit 8): using a reading passage to defend an idea; using formulaic expressions to check understanding; referring to others' ideas
Hafta 9: Academic Speaking (Unit 8): giving a short pair presentation using a reading passage to defend an idea

Hafta 10: Academic Reading (Unit 5): identifying main ideas in a passage; identifying key features of a summary; evaluating a passage summary; critical thinking about ideas in writing; Academic Writing (Unit 5): identifying and using words and expressions used in summaries; summarizing a paragraph

Hafta 11: Academic Reading (Unit 6): examining article titles; understanding a longer source text; identifying the author's approach to main ideas; identifying details in supporting ideas in a passage

Hafta 12: Academic Reading (Unit 6): analyzing patterns and theories in a passage; identifying specific ideas in a passage; understanding comparisons in a passage; use of comparison and contrast; recognizing the author's tone, purpose, and credibility

Hafta 13: Academic Reading (Unit 10): identifying arguments in a long passage; understanding adjectives used for evaluation; identifying the conclusion and premise in an argument; evaluating the strength of the conclusion and premise; evaluating arguments in writing and expressing personal opinions
Hafta 14: General review; presentations and make-up exams
Hafta 15: Final exam
resources: In house materials – Copy centre or Google Classroom

courses.name: Critical and Analytical Thinking Fundamentals
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' analytical thinking skills through interactive problems, critical evaluation of arguments, and engaging scenarios.
courses.description: Students will develop the ability to analyze propositions and logical reasoning in various contexts. The course also includes the explanation of artistic productions in chronological order with visuals, situating them within period-based approaches in terms of technical and content aspects, analyzing artworks in technical, content, and iconographic contexts, and evaluating an artwork within the framework of period-specific aesthetic discourses and values.
learning_outcomes: Define different types of logical statements.
Evaluate the validity and soundness of arguments.
Distinguish common cognitive biases and heuristic thinking errors.
Apply principles of logical reasoning to solve complex problems.
Apply logical reasoning to discuss a given dilemma.

Hafta 2: Module 1: Introduction to Analytical Thinking Basic Logical Statements and the Wason Card Selection Task Types of Propositions: Statements, Assumptions, and Facts Structure of Arguments: Claims, Evidence, and Conclusions
Hafta 3: Module 2: Types of Arguments and Logical Fallacies Deductive and Inductive Arguments
Hafta 4: Module 2: Types of Arguments and Logical Fallacies Common Logical Fallacies: Ad Hominem, Straw Man, Slippery Slope, Using AI tools
Hafta 5: Module 3: Evaluating Arguments Criteria for Evaluating Arguments: Validity, Soundness, and Strength
Hafta 6: Module 3: Evaluating Arguments Criteria for Evaluating Arguments: Validity, Soundness, and Strength

Hafta 8: Module 4: Intuitions and Cognitive Biases Cognitive Biases: Confirmation Bias, Overconfidence, Hindsight Bias
Hafta 9: Module 5: Analysis of Dilemmas and Ethical Reasoning Types of Dilemmas: Moral, Practical, and Ethical
Hafta 10: Module 5: Analysis of Dilemmas and Ethical Reasoning Ethical Decision-Making Frameworks: Utilitarianism, Deontology Ethical Dilemma: "Whose Life Is More Valuable?"
Hafta 11: Module 6: Logical and Analytical Reasoning Techniques Five simple strategies to sharpen your critical thinking skills Computational Thinking

courses.name: İŞLETME MATEMATİĞİ II
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, işletme ve ekonomi alanlarında karşılaşılan problemleri analiz edebilmek için ileri düzey matematiksel kavramları tanıtmak ve bu kavramları kullanarak nicel modeller geliştirme yetkinliği kazandırmaktır. Öğrencilerin, türev, integral ve çok değişkenli fonksiyonlar gibi kalkülüs konularını öğrenerek; talep esnekliği, optimizasyon, maliyet-gelir analizi gibi iş dünyasına özgü uygulamaları çözümleyebilecek matematiksel düşünme ve problem çözme becerileri edinmeleri hedeflenmektedir.
courses.description: Bu ders, ilişkili oranlar, talep esnekliği, türev ve integral hesaplamaları, optimizasyon yöntemleri ve çok değişkenli fonksiyonlar gibi temel kalkülüs konularını kapsar. Amaç, işletme ve ekonomi alanlarında karşılaşılan problemleri nicel olarak modelleyebilmek ve bu modelleri analiz etmek için gerekli matematiksel araçları öğretmektir. Öğrenciler, özellikle türev ve integral kavramlarını kullanarak marjinal analiz, alan hesabı, gelir-maliyet değerlendirmesi ve optimizasyon gibi uygulamalı problemleri çözme yetkinliği kazanırlar.
learning_outcomes: İşletme ve ekonomi problemlerinde kullanılan matematiksel modelleri oluşturur.
Birinci ve ikinci türevleri kullanarak grafik analizleri yapar.
Talep esnekliği ve ilişkili oranlar gibi kavramları hesaplar.
Belirli ve belirsiz integralleri uygular.
Çok değişkenli fonksiyonlarda kısmi türev alma becerisini kullanır.

courses.name: DESIGN THINKING FUNDAMENTALS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce design thinking as a creative and interdisciplinary method in both theoretical and practical terms, enabling students to use this approach effectively in entrepreneurship, innovation, and problem-solving processes. The course treats design not only as the production of tangible objects, but also as a system of thinking and a way of acting, supporting students in creating meaningful impact in different contexts.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental stages of the design thinking process: understanding, observation/empathy, problem definition, ideation, prototyping, and testing. Students work in teams composed of different disciplines and experience applying this approach to real-world problems. In addition, the course includes discussions on creative collaboration culture and self-organizing team structures in the future.
learning_outcomes: Uses design thinking principles to generate innovative ideas and creative solutions.
Collaborates effectively with team members from different disciplines.
Applies rapid prototyping and iteration to improve proposals.
Applies design thinking to solve problems with user-centered and creative approaches.
Creates low-fidelity prototypes to quickly test and validate ideas.

courses.name: GİRİŞİMCİLİĞİN TEMELLERİ
courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilere girişimci bir şekilde düşünmek ve hareket etmek için gerekli temel bilgi ve becerileri kazandırmayı amaçlamaktadır. Ders, yaratıcılık, problem çözme ve fırsatları fark etme becerilerini geliştirerek girişimci bir zihniyetin oluşmasını destekler. Öğrenciler, pazar ihtiyaçlarını belirlemeyi, değer odaklı çözümler üretmeyi ve bir girişimin kurulması ve yönetilmesindeki zorlukların üstesinden gelmeyi öğreneceklerdir. Dersin sonunda öğrenciler, fikirlerini uygulanabilir iş konseptlerine dönüştürebilecek ve bunları etkili bir şekilde aktarabileceklerdir.
courses.description: Bu ders, öğrencilere fırsatları fark etme, fikir üretme, iş modeli oluşturma ve pazar analizi gibi temel girişimcilik kavramlarını tanıtmaktadır. Finansal temeller, stratejik planlama ve finansman seçeneklerini kapsayan ders, girişimlerin geliştirilmesi için kapsamlı bir temel sağlamaktadır. Etkileşimli alıştırmalar ve vaka çalışmalarıyla öğrenciler, iş fikirlerini geliştirecek, yapılandırılmış bir iş planı oluşturacak ve paydaşları ve potansiyel yatırımcıları çekmek için sunum tekniklerini öğreneceklerdir.
learning_outcomes: Fırsatları belirlemek ve yenilikçi iş fikirleri üretmek için eleştirel düşünme, yaratıcılık ve problem çözme becerilerini uygular.
İş modeli, değer yaratma, müşteri geliştirme ve pazar dinamikleri gibi temel girişimcilik kavramlarını analiz ederek iş fikirlerini değerlendirir ve geliştirir.
Girişimcilik projeleri için temel finansal bilgileri kullanarak bir iş planı hazırlar ve finansman kaynaklarını belirler.
İş fikirlerini etkili biçimde sunar; hikâyeleştirme teknikleri, görsel araçlar ve yatırımcı odaklı sunum becerilerini kullanır.
Farklı iş bağlamlarında girişimcilik kararlarının etik, sosyal ve çevresel etkilerini değerlendirir.

courses.name: Principles of Management
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles, concepts, and functions of management. Students examine the roles of managers, decision-making processes, planning, organizing, leadership, control mechanisms, and strategic thinking. Through case analyses, interviews with managers, and real-life examples, the course also explores how management practices are shaped by ethics, technological change, and innovation, while developing analytical thinking, communication, and leadership skills.
courses.description: The course covers core topics in contemporary management, including the qualities of managers, managing internal and external organizational environments, managerial decision-making, planning and strategic management, management of start-up companies, innovation and change, organizational structure and team design, leadership, and communication.
learning_outcomes: Explains the basic concepts and principles related to the management process.
Evaluates different management functions such as planning, organizing, leadership, and control with examples.
Interprets decision-making, communication, and leadership skills by applying them to case analyses.
Analyzes management processes from the perspectives of ethics, social responsibility, and digital transformation.
Produces insights into effective management by comparing managerial practices across different sectors.

Hafta 2: Managerial Roles and Current Challenges
Hafta 3: External and Internal Management Environment

Hafta 6: Fundamentals of Planning and Strategic Management
Hafta 7: Organization: Designing a Business
Hafta 8: Human Resources and Talent Management

courses.name: ACADEMIC ENGLISH II
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop and reinforce students' academic skills through regular attendance and practice. Students will improve their ability to read and understand academic texts, conduct research, analyze sources, paraphrase and summarize texts, take notes and comment on ideas, write critical responses, and discuss topics orally by supporting their views with evidence.
courses.description: This course uses an integrated process- and product-oriented program to help students acquire and reinforce the academic skills and strategies needed to critique academic texts and discuss ideas in groups. The course covers reading, understanding academic texts, research skills, pre-writing, paraphrasing, summarizing, writing critical responses, and discussing opinions orally in group settings.
learning_outcomes: Remembers the structure of an organization.
Remembers the structure of an extended paragraph.
Discusses the process of choosing a topic for paragraphs and narrowing the topic to produce a focus.
Recognizes the key characteristics and stages of academic writing.
Knows what plagiarism is and ways to avoid it.
Remembers ways of quoting and summarizing.
Uses practical quotation inclusion, quotation in writing, quoting and summarizing.
Uses the YU resource center and the internet efficiently to find valid, current, and reliable sources.
Identifies valid, current, and reliable sources for research.
Analyzes/inquires sources found according to their validity and reliability, and recognizes different referencing systems.
Knows and applies APA rules.
Determines how to include each section in an extended paragraph from the sources found.
Uses the sources found to write a critical response. Edits and writes the final version of the paragraph according to proper organization and citation rules.
Students will be able to interpret and evaluate texts and think more critically.
Collects information from different sources; cites correctly; understands and analyzes texts.
Learns to paraphrase, summarize, plan, organize, and write a critical response.
Reviews and rewrites assignments and contributes academically to group discussions.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Recognizing the course objectives and classroom rules. Reflecting on writing skills. Simple sentence structures, correcting faulty complex sentences, writing proper compound sentences, analyzing paragraph structure, writing the main idea, coherence in paragraphs, using pronouns and determiners for cohesion, connecting the concluding sentence to the main idea, analyzing concluding sentences, writing concluding sentences.

Hafta 2: Examining the structure of a paragraph. Achieving paragraph coherence with conjunctions. Linking sentences to form a paragraph. Adding details to sentences. Planning and writing a paragraph. Evaluating the student's written paragraph. Understanding academic conventions.
Hafta 3: Academic writing: Avoiding plagiarism: Direct quotation.
Hafta 4: Academic writing: Ways to avoid plagiarism: Paraphrasing.
Hafta 5: Academic writing: Ways to avoid plagiarism: Summarizing. Midterm review.
Hafta 6: Introduction to writing a critical response. Midterm exam.
Hafta 7: Introduction to the critical essay. Writing the critical essay: brainstorming. Research techniques.
Hafta 8: Writing the critical essay: preparing the essay outline.
Hafta 9: Instructor feedback on the essay outline; submission of the outline. Writing the critical essay: drafting.
Hafta 10: Writing the critical essay: reviewing and revising the draft. Submission of the critical essay draft.
Hafta 11: Instructor feedback on the critical essay draft. How to discuss? Students watch a sample discussion video, read other students' papers, and begin generating new ideas for discussion.
Hafta 12: Submission of the final critical essay. Reading other students' essays.
Hafta 13: Discussion rehearsals.
Hafta 14: Discussion exam.
Hafta 15: Discussion and make-up midterm.

courses.name: OBJECT-ORIENTED PROGRAMMING
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid foundation in Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) using Java and to equip them with the skills needed to design, develop, and implement efficient software solutions. Through a combination of theoretical concepts and practical applications, students will gain hands-on experience with exception handling, file I/O operations, generics, collections, GUI development, and design patterns while exploring core OOP principles such as encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction.
courses.description: The course begins with an introduction to Object-Oriented Programming and Java, covering fundamental concepts such as classes, objects, and methods. It then delves into core OOP principles including encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction with practical examples. Students will also explore Java generics, collections, UML-based software design, and exception handling. The second part of the course covers file I/O operations, recursion, data structures, and GUI programming. Finally, students will examine software design patterns and review key concepts to strengthen their understanding and application of Object-Oriented Programming.
learning_outcomes: Explain Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts, core principles such as encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and common design patterns.
Develop Java programs using classes, objects, methods, and UML-based modeling techniques.
Implement Java applications involving exception handling and file input/output (I/O) operations.
Apply Java generics and the Collections Framework to build structured programming solutions.
Design basic graphical user interfaces using standard Java GUI components.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming. Java programming language. Setting up the IDE.
Hafta 2: Basic object-oriented design: Class, Object, Method.
Hafta 3: Encapsulation/Abstraction concepts. Examples.
Hafta 4: Inheritance: Concepts. Examples.
Hafta 5: Polymorphism: Concepts. Examples.
Hafta 6: Java general programming collections: Basic concepts. Examples.
Hafta 7: UML Design (1/2): UML design in Java.
Hafta 8: UML Design (2/2) & Review: UML design in Java. Midterm review.
Hafta 9: Exception handling and examples.
Hafta 10: Streams and File I/O and examples.
Hafta 11: Recursions, Data structures.
Hafta 12: GUI Programming (1/2): Introduction to GUI in Java.
Hafta 13: GUI Programming (2/2): GUI examples and more.
Hafta 14: Design Patterns and Review.

courses.name: STATISTICAL ANALYSIS
courses.purpose: This course aims to create students' awareness of basic statistical concepts.
courses.description: Basic concepts used in statistics. Variables and variable types. Frequency table. Measures of central tendency and dispersion. Graphical representation. Introduction to probability. Random variable. Joint probability distribution of a discrete random variable. Some discrete and continuous probability distributions. Sampling distributions. Confidence intervals for a population mean, a population proportion, and the difference between two population means (when population variances are known). Hypothesis testing for a population mean (population variance known and unknown). Hypothesis testing for a population proportion. Hypothesis testing for the difference between two population means (when population variances are known).
learning_outcomes: Students can summarize and interpret data sets using statistical methods.
Students can explain basic probability theory and make inferences under uncertainty.
Students can recognize common discrete and continuous probability distributions and use them appropriately in application areas.
Students can make data-driven decisions using appropriate statistical analysis methods.
Students can formulate and test hypotheses.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Statistics. Descriptive Statistics
Hafta 2: Descriptive Statistics
Hafta 3: Descriptive Statistics
Hafta 4: Introduction to Probability
Hafta 5: Introduction to Probability
Hafta 6: Mean, variance, and standard deviation of a discrete probability distribution. Joint probability distribution of discrete random variables.
Hafta 7: Some commonly used discrete probability distributions
Hafta 8: Some commonly used continuous probability distributions
Hafta 9: Sampling and sampling distribution.
Hafta 10: Confidence interval for population mean (population variance known)
Hafta 11: Confidence interval for population mean (population variance unknown). Confidence interval for population proportion.
Hafta 12: Concept of hypothesis testing. Hypothesis tests for the mean of a normal distribution (sigma known)
Hafta 13: Hypothesis tests for the mean of a normal distribution (sigma unknown)
Hafta 14: Hypothesis tests for the difference between two normal population means. Hypothesis tests for population proportion
resources: Paul Newbold, William L. Carlson, Betty Thorne. Statistics for Business and Economics, Prentice Hall.

courses.name: Data Structures and File Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an understanding of algorithms and data structures, focusing on their design, analysis, and implementation. Students will learn to choose the most appropriate data structures for different problem-solving scenarios, understand trade-offs among approaches, and gain hands-on experience in coding and optimizing these structures. In addition, the course examines how data structures are represented in memory, with emphasis on efficient data processing through reading, writing, searching, and sorting algorithms.
courses.description: This course covers the design, analysis, and implementation of fundamental and several advanced data structures in Java. Students learn how to effectively select and implement data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, and hash tables, while developing a deep understanding of their time and space complexity. The course also addresses algorithmic strategies such as divide and conquer, greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, and backtracking, with a focus on solving real-world computational problems. Topics include data representation in memory and on disk, file processing, and effective searching, sorting, and optimization techniques.
learning_outcomes: Define and implement fundamental data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, and explain their properties, use cases, and algorithmic efficiency.
Understand and examine file organization methods and the internal representation of data structures in memory.
Apply and understand the principles behind searching, sorting, and traversal algorithms, and recognize which data structures effectively support these operations through coding in Java.
Understand how indexing and hashing support fast data retrieval and efficient file management.
Implement and interpret basic file processing and data storage mechanisms.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: INTRODUCTION AND REVIEW: Problem-solving techniques. Overview of algorithm analysis. Time and space complexity analysis. Data structures. Java basics for algorithms and data structures.

Hafta 2: STORAGE: Storage and memory. In-memory data representation. Serialization and deserialization of data. Space-time trade-offs. Memory management strategies. Overview of design techniques: divide and conquer, greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, backtracking.
Hafta 3: ADT AND ARRAY: ADT. Single arrays and multidimensional arrays. Array processing. Advantages and disadvantages.
Hafta 4: BASIC DATA STRUCTURE: STACK: Stacks. Stack operations. Infix postfix. Advantages and disadvantages.
Hafta 5: BASIC DATA STRUCTURE: QUEUE: Queues. Queue operations. Advantages and disadvantages.
Hafta 6: BASIC DATA STRUCTURE: LINKED LIST: Linear linked list, linked stack, linked queue, unlinked list. Resizing arrays. General. Advantages and disadvantages.
Hafta 7: ALGORITHM PROBLEM SOLVING: SORTING: Sorting algorithms (insertion, selection, bubble sort). Complexity analysis for data operations.
Hafta 8: GENERAL REVIEW & MIDTERM EXAM
Hafta 9: ALGORITHM PROBLEM SOLVING: SORTING: Sorting algorithms (insertion, selection, bubble sort). Complexity analysis for data operations.

Hafta 10: TREES: Binary tree. Representing lists as binary trees, trees and their applications; tree search, tree traversals, insertion and deletion. Binary search trees (BST): structure, properties and operations (insertion, deletion, search). AVL trees and balancing techniques.

Hafta 11: GRAPHS: Graph fundamentals; representation, traversals and applications. Shortest path algorithms: Dijkstra and Bellman-Ford. Minimum spanning tree: Kruskal and Prim algorithms. Topological sorting. Depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS). Graph problems and solutions in Java.

Hafta 12: HASHING: Overview of hashing and hash functions. Hash table concepts: collision handling (chaining, open addressing). Practical applications of hashing. Time and space complexity of hashing.

Hafta 13: WORKING WITH FILES AND DATABASES: File processing in Java (reading, writing and updating files). Data structures in databases. Indexing and query optimization. File and database compression techniques.
Hafta 14: GENERAL REVIEW: Course objectives. Exams and assessments.
resources: Data structures and algorithms in Java, M. Goodrich, R. Tamassia, 2014, 6th Edition, Wiley, ISBN 978-0-470-39880-7.
Algorithms, 4th Edition, Robert Sedgewick and Kevin Wayne, Princeton University https://algs4.cs.princeton.edu/lectures/

courses.name: PYTHON İLE İŞLETME ANALİTİĞİNE GİRİŞ
courses.purpose: İş analitiğinin temellerini açıklayarak, sektörden bağımsız olarak işletme verileri üzerinde iş içgörüleri elde etmek amacıyla veri ön işleme, özellik inceleme ve model seçimi de dahil olmak üzere uçtan uca bir veri analizi yürütebilmek.
courses.description: Bu ders, öğrencilere veri yoğun iş uygulamalarında karşılaşılan çeşitli veri analizi sorunlarına ilişkin temel bir anlayış kazandıracaktır. Öğrenciler, çeşitli veri kümelerini kullanarak veri temizleme, düzenleme, analiz, raporlama ve görselleştirme üzerinde çalışacak ve Python programlama alıştırmaları yapacaklardır.
learning_outcomes: İş analitiği kategorilerinin temellerini açıklamak
İşletmeler için uygulanabilir içgörüler elde etmek amacıyla, veri ön işleme, özellik mühendisliği ve model seçimi de dahil olmak üzere uçtan uca bir veri hattı yürütmek
Sektör standardı araçları kullanarak gelişmiş veri analizi iş akışlarını uygulamak
Gerçek dünya iş sorunlarına veri odaklı çözümler geliştirmek
Karmaşık analitik bulguları ilgi çekici veri öykülerine (Görselleştirmeler/Gösterge Panelleri) dönüştürmek ve veriye dayalı kararların etik sonuçlarını (önyargı, gizlilik, adalet) eleştirmek

Hafta 2: Python ve Google Colab'a Giriş, Sözdizimi, Değişkenler, Operatörler, Giriş/Çıkış, Temel Hesaplamalar, Dizilerin/Listelerin Neden Yeterli Olmadığı. Vektörleştirme. DataFrame'e Giriş.
Hafta 3: Veri Yönetimi, Veri Hazırlığı (Veri, Veri Ön İşleme), Veri Düzenleme ve Temizleme, Veri biliminin "80/20" kuralı. Eksik değerler, tekrarlanan kayıtlar ve aykırı değerlerle başa çıkma.
Hafta 4: Keşifsel Veri Analizi (EDA), Görsel algı. Verilerle hikaye anlatımı. Dağılımlar ve korelasyonlar. (Veri Keşfi, Veri Sınıflandırması)
Hafta 5: Tanımlayıcı İstatistik ve Olasılık, Merkezi Eğilim, Varyans, A/B Testi kavramları.
Hafta 6: Makine Öğrenimine Giriş, Denetimli ve Denetimsiz Öğrenme. Modelleme İş Akışı (Böl-Eğit-Test).
Hafta 7: Veri Sınıflandırması - Pazarlama, Finans Örnekleri, Veri Kümelemesi - Pazarlama ve Üretim Örnekleri
Hafta 8: Arasınav
Hafta 9: Regresyon Analizi, Basit ve Çoklu Doğ?? Regresyon. Katsayıların Anlaşılması (Yorumlanabilirlik).
Hafta 10: Sınıflandırma I, Lojistik Regresyon. Olasılıksal sınıflandırma. İkili sonuçlar. Metin Analizi
Hafta 11: Sınıflandırma II, Karar Ağaçları ve Rastgele Ormanlar. Doğrusal olmayan ilişkilerin ele alınması.
Hafta 12: Kümeleme (Denetimsiz), K-Means algoritması. Verilerdeki "Mesafeleri" Anlamak. Nesnelerin İnterneti'nin Veri Analitiği ile Entegrasyonu
Hafta 13: Model Değerlendirme ve Ayarlama, Hassasiyet ve Geri Çağırma Arasındaki Fark. ROC Eğrisi. Aşırı Uyum ve Yetersiz Uyum. Yapılandırılmamış Veri (Metin Madenciliği), Duygu Analizi Kavramları.

courses.purpose: This is a one-semester undergraduate accounting course for non-business students. It introduces the role of accounting in business and management and enables students to understand basic accounting concepts and apply them in recording, preparing, and interpreting accounting information.

In this context, basic accounting principles and concepts, the accounting cycle, and the preparation of financial statements are covered in detail.

Explain generally accepted accounting principles by applying them to basic financial statements
Identify and apply the steps in the accounting cycle

Define the main groups and related items of financial statements (e.g., current assets and liabilities, equity, cost of goods sold expenses and revenues, cash flows from operating, investing, and financing activities)
Prepare basic financial statements and explain the relationship among them
